bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to concede much, so I am targeting a very high-utility opening bid for myself. The opponent’s opening offer gives me low utility and their message is aggressive cheap talk rather than evidence of real constraints, so I will anchor near my best outcome while staying within the valid domain.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid while making a small concession to avoid simply repeating my prior offer. The opponent’s offers are still very poor for me and have not improved; their aggressive messages look like pressure tactics rather than evidence of real concession. I concede only on low-impact issueE, moving from my top valueB to nearly equivalent valueA.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making a visible but limited concession. The opponent’s offers remain very poor for me and mostly fixed around valueE, with only slight movement on issueC/issueD, so I will concede first on a low-weight issue they consistently request rather than weakening my high-weight issues.

I am still targeting a high-utility bid because it is very early in the negotiation, but I am making a clearer concession on low-weight issueD while holding firm on my highest-value issues A, B, and C. The opponent’s offers remain clustered around valueE and have only modestly improved for me, so their aggressive messages look like pressure rather than evidence that I should make major early concessions.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.80 because it is very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain low-value to me and show little real concession despite aggressive messages, so I concede mainly on lower-weight issues while holding firm on issueA, issueB, and issueC.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid for me while making a visible concession on the lower-weight issues D and E. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, and their actual offers remain very low utility to me with only limited movement, so I am holding firm on my most important issues A, B, and C.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.76 since it is early at t=0.12, but I am making visible concessions on lower-importance issues rather than giving up my most important issueA. The opponent’s latest valid offer improved for me mainly by moving issueC to valueB, while they continue to push valueE-like outcomes elsewhere; their aggressive messages are cheap talk, but the bidding history suggests I can concede on lower-weight issueD and issueE first.

I am targeting a still-high utility around 0.75 because it is early at t=0.14 and the opponent’s actual offers have not shown consistent concession despite aggressive messages. I will hold firm on my high-weight issues A, B, and C, while conceding on lower-weight issues D and E toward values the opponent has repeatedly offered, avoiding an exact repeat of my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility bid because it is early at t=0.16 and the opponent’s actual offers remain very low for me despite their aggressive messages. Their only meaningful movement has been occasional concessions on issueC, issueD, and issueE, while they keep demanding movement on my high-weight issueA and issueB; I will hold firm there and concede on lower-weight issues to avoid repeating my last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.70 because it is early in the negotiation, but I should show a meaningful move after holding firm on the high-importance issues. The opponent’s bids and messages show they are especially pressing on issueA and issueB, but their actual offers remain very poor for me overall; I will concede moderately on issueB and low-weight issues while keeping my top positions on issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.69, since we are only at t=0.20 and there is no need to concede heavily yet. The opponent’s bids remain low-value for me and their messages are mostly pressure tactics, but they have signaled repeated interest in concessions on issueB and the lower-weight issues, so I am conceding on issueB, issueD, and issueE while holding firm on my highest-value issueA and issueC positions.

I am targeting a utility around 0.67, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting my most important interests at this early-mid stage. The opponent’s offers remain very low utility for me and their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their repeated pressure on issueA/issueB suggests that conceding on issueA may be the most credible signal without giving up too much too soon.

I targeted a moderate utility around 0.60, making a meaningful concession while still protecting my highest-weight interests. Since it is still relatively early at t=0.24, I am not matching the opponent’s very low-utility bids, but their repeated pressure on issueA and issueB suggests movement there may be most useful. I am holding issueC at my best value because they have already offered valueB there before, while conceding lower-weight issueD and issueE toward values they often propose.

At t=0.26 I am still targeting a solid positive utility rather than rushing to the opponent’s low-utility proposals, since their actual offers remain quite poor for me despite their aggressive messages. I am making a measured concession on issueB, which they have repeatedly emphasized, while holding stronger positions on my most important issueA and issueC and already conceding lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a moderate-high utility bid around 0.65, since we are still before the midpoint and there is no need to drop to the opponent’s low-utility proposals yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their offers show they strongly favor concessions on issueB, issueD, and issueE, while their latest bid conceded issueA to my top value; I keep issueA and issueC strong while moving issueB/D/E toward their pattern.

I am targeting a moderate-concession bid with raw weighted utility around 0.63, appropriate for t=0.30 where there is still time but some movement is useful. The opponent’s messages are mostly pressure tactics, but their offers show some willingness to move on issueA and issueC while consistently pushing hard on issueB/issueD/issueE, so I concede on lower-weight issueD/issueE and partially on issueB while holding my high-value issueA and issueC positions.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.69, conceding meaningfully on lower/medium-priority issues while holding issueA and issueC because they are most valuable to me. The opponent’s actual offers have improved somewhat, especially by offering issueB: valueB and issueC: valueB recently, but their messages are mostly pressure tactics, so I am responding to the bid pattern rather than the rhetoric.

I am targeting a weighted utility of about 0.58, a meaningful concession from my earlier bids while still protecting my higher-weight issues. At t=0.34 there is some pressure to move, and the opponent’s offers show gradual concessions, especially recently on issueA, though their messages are aggressive cheap talk rather than binding evidence. I concede on issueA to valueB and give low-weight issueD/issueE as valueE, while holding issueC at my top value and keeping issueB at a moderate compromise.

I am targeting a moderate-utility counter around 0.51: lower than my recent offers but still clearly above reservation, which is appropriate at t=0.36 without overconceding too early. The opponent’s bids have improved for me recently, especially by offering issueA:valueB and issueC:valueB, but they still push hard on issueB and the low-weight issues, so I concede issueD/issueE and move issueB only partway toward them. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but the actual offers show gradual movement rather than a true final position.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.63, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent’s latest offer has become materially better for me by conceding issueA and issueC. Their messages are aggressive cheap talk, but the offer history shows real movement on issueA and issueC while they still seem to press hard on issueB, issueD, and issueE, so I will hold my high-value issueA/issueC positions and make concessions on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.65, preserving my highest-weight priorities on issueA and issueC while making concessions on lower-weight issueD and keeping issueB at a value the opponent has repeatedly used. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their offers show they have at times accepted issueA=valueA, issueC=valueB, issueB=valueD, and recently issueE=valueC, so this combines signaled feasible values without over-conceding at t=0.40.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.60: enough concession to move from my earlier high-utility bids while still protecting the most important issueA and issueC positions. The opponent’s actual offers have improved only unevenly and their messages are mostly pressure tactics, but they have signaled possible flexibility on issueA/valueB, issueB/valueC, and issueC/valueB, so I am conceding more on lower-weight issueD and issueE.

At t=0.44 I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.60 while making a visible mid-game concession package rather than holding my opening positions. The opponent’s latest bid moved substantially on issueA and issueB but stayed poor for me on issueC, so I will accept their movement on A/B and concede the lower-weight issueD/issueE, while holding issueC at my top value.
